title: Board game session record (core)
description: Cross-game fields. Validated against every record in addition to the game's variant schema.

game.description: Lowercase hyphenated game slug, optionally ending with a '.YYYY' year suffix to disambiguate distinct games sharing the same printed title (e.g. 'coup', 'coup.1988').

variant.description: 'base' or an expansion slug. Variants cannot carry a year suffix and cannot be four digits alone — this keeps the <game>.<variant> split unambiguous against game slugs carrying a .YYYY suffix.

player_count.description: Number of seated players. Must equal len(players).

winners.description: Indices into players[] of the winner(s). Multiple entries = tie or team win.

notes.description: Free-form prose for details the structured fields don't capture.

players.description: One entry per player, in seating order. Position in array = player index.

name.description: Player's name/handle. Non-unique is fine; winners references by index.

email.description: Optional stable identifier for the player across sessions. Used for aggregations and stats over time.

identity.description: Colour / faction / civ / mat / role. Variant schemas may constrain via enum.

team.description: Team number for team games. Teammates share the value.

eliminated.description: Whether the player was knocked out before the game ended. Omit for games without elimination.

end_state.description: Map of everything the player holds at game end. Values are integers for counts, booleans for flags (e.g. longest_road). Keys and per-key types are constrained per variant.
